title: 10 best herbal teas for eye bags
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye bags are infusions made from plants that can help reduce the appearance of eye bags and dark circles. They typically contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can soothe and tighten the skin around the eyes. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for eye bags include chamomile, green tea, and rosehip tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness, while green tea contains caffeine and antioxidants that can stimulate circulation and decrease fluid buildup. Rosehip tea is rich in vitamin C, which can help brighten and firm the skin. Drinking these herbal teas or using them as a compress can improve the appearance of eye bags, making people look more refreshed and well-rested. This can boost their confidence and self-esteem, and positively impact their social and professional interactions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with eye bags because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the puffiness and discoloration under the eyes. The caffeine in white tea can also help to constrict blood vessels, which can improve circulation and reduce the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, white tea is high in tannins and catechins, which can help to strengthen and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the likelihood of developing eye bags in the future. Drinking white tea or applying white tea bags as a compress can both provide benefits for reducing eye bags.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the puffiness and swelling of eye bags, thanks to its high concentration of flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants, such as epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which can protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, helping to reduce the appearance of eye bags.

Anti-aging: White tea has anti-aging properties that can help improve the elasticity and firmness of the skin, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles. This is due to its high levels of polyphenols and catechins.

Vasoconstrictor: White tea contains vasoconstrictor properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce the appearance of eye bags by improving circulation and reducing fluid buildup. This is attributed to its theophylline and theobromine content.
Soothing: White tea has soothing properties that can help calm and soothe the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. This is due to its high content of flavonols and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and 1 teaspoon of dried calendula petals.
Combine the herbs in a heat-resistant container and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them.
Allow the mixture to steep, covered, for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the tea and place them over closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with eye bags because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The menthol present in peppermint leaves can improve blood circulation, which helps in reducing the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, its cooling effect can help alleviate tiredness and stress, further contributing to a refreshed and rejuvenated look. Drinking peppermint tea or using it as a compress can provide a natural and gentle solution to combat eye bags and promote a well-rested appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the skin around the eyes, which can help alleviate the appearance of eye bags. This property is due to the presence of menthol and rosmarinic acid.

Vasoconstrictor: Peppermint tea can help tighten blood vessels and reduce the pooling of fluid under the eyes, which can help reduce the appearance of eye bags. This is attributed to the menthol content.

Antioxidant: Peppermint tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage and promote skin health, potentially reducing the appearance of eye bags. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Calming: Peppermint tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help soothe irritation and reduce puffiness around the eyes. This property can be attributed to the menthol and flavonoids present in peppermint.
Diuretic: Peppermint tea has diuretic properties, which can help reduce fluid retention and minimize the appearance of eye bags. This is due to the presence of menthol and other volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the peppermint leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the tea and place them over closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with eye bags because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. Chamomile is also known for its calming and soothing effects, which can help alleviate stress and tension that can contribute to eye bags. The tea can be used topically by soaking two tea bags in hot water, letting them cool, and then placing them over closed eyes for about 10-15 minutes. The antioxidants and flavonoids found in chamomile can help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and strengthen the skin around the eyes, making it look healthier and more youthful. Additionally, drinking chamomile tea can also help promote relaxation and better sleep, which can contribute to reducing eye bags caused by lack of sleep and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags. This is due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.

Antioxidant: It contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of eye bags. The antioxidants include apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin.

Anti-spasmodic: German chamomile tea has anti-spasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and strain that can contribute to eye bags. This is attributed to the presence of bisabolol.
Astringent: It has astringent properties that can help tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags. This is due to its tannin content.
Soothing: German chamomile tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with eye bags. This is attributed to the presence of azulene.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature, ensuring it's not too hot for your skin.
Soak two clean washcloths in the chamomile tea, wringing out excess liquid.
Place the soaked washcloths on closed eyelids for 10-15 minutes to reduce eye bags.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with eye bags because it contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The tea also has astringent properties that can help tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of eye bags. Furthermore, pot marigold tea is rich in antioxidants that can improve blood circulation and reduce the dark circles that often accompany eye bags. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a compress can help soothe and rejuvenate the delicate skin around the eyes, giving you a refreshed and well-rested appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for eye bags as they are often caused by fluid retention and inflammation in the eye area. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for eye bags as oxidative stress can contribute to the formation of under-eye bags. The active constituents responsible for this property include lutein, zeaxanthin, and vitamin C.

Astringent: Pot marigold tea has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the skin, which is important for eye bags as it can help reduce the appearance of puffiness and dark circles. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Diuretic: Pot marigold tea has diuretic properties that help reduce fluid retention, which is important for eye bags as they are often caused by excess fluid buildup in the eye area. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.

Soothing: Pot marigold tea has soothing properties that help reduce redness and irritation, which is important for eye bags as they can be caused by a variety of factors, including allergies and lack of sleep. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried marigold flowers and 1 cup of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then pour it over the marigold flowers.
Steep the marigold flowers in the hot water for 10 minutes.
Strain the marigold flowers from the tea, discarding the solids.
Allow the marigold tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before using it as a compress for eye bags.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with eye bags because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The tea is rich in flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can help improve blood circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and promote better sleep, both of which can contribute to the development of eye bags. Drinking littleleaf linden tea or using it as a compress can help soothe and relax the area around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and leaving you looking refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area, thanks to its active constituents, flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content helps protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye bags, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Diuretic: Littleleaf linden tea acts as a natural diuretic, helping to reduce fluid retention and puffiness in the eye area, as a result of its active constituents, including flavonoids and tannins.

Calming: The soothing properties of littleleaf linden tea help alleviate stress and promote relaxation, which can reduce eye bags caused by lack of sleep or fatigue, owing to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and essential oils.

Vasoconstrictor: Littleleaf linden tea contains vasoconstrictor properties that help narrow blood vessels, reducing redness and puffiness in the eye area, due to its active constituents, including tannins and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the herbs.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using it as a compress on eye bags.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with eye bags because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The tea is rich in antioxidants and bioflavonoids that help improve blood circulation, reduce fluid retention, and strengthen the capillaries around the eyes. Additionally, the tea has a cooling and soothing effect that can help alleviate eye strain and fatigue. Drinking maidenhair tree tea or using it as a compress can help reduce the appearance of eye bags, making you look refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree tea reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate swelling and puffiness in eye bags. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of maidenhair tree tea protect skin cells from damage, promoting healthy skin around the eyes and reducing the appearance of eye bags. The primary antioxidants in maidenhair tree tea are quercetin, kaempferol, and myricetin.

Diuretic: Maidenhair tree tea promotes fluid elimination, helping to reduce excess fluid buildup under the eyes. This diuretic effect is attributed to the presence of caffeine and other xanthine alkaloids.

Collagen synthesis promotion: Maidenhair tree tea supports collagen production, which improves skin elasticity and reduces the appearance of eye bags. The active compounds responsible for this property are primarily flavonoids and triterpenes.
Vascular constriction: Maidenhair tree tea constricts blood vessels, reducing redness and swelling in the eye area. This property is attributed to the caffeine content in the tea.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried maidenhair tree leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose leaf tea bag.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes to reach a temperature of 175-185°F.
Pour the hot water over the maidenhair tree leaves and let them steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag and allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the herbal tea and place them on closed eyelids for 10-15 minutes to help reduce eye bags.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bilberry tea helps with eye bags because it contains anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants that strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation. This can reduce the appearance of eye bags by reducing inflammation and fluid buildup under the eyes. Additionally, bilberry tea is rich in vitamin C and other nutrients that can help to brighten and tighten the skin, further reducing the appearance of eye bags. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, providing a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bilberry tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce eye puffiness by decreasing swelling and irritation, thanks to anthocyanins and tannins.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in bilberry tea protects the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye bags, due to flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Anti-diabetic: Bilberry tea's ability to regulate blood sugar levels can prevent fluid retention, a common cause of eye bags, due to the anthocyanosides content.
Venotonic: Bilberry tea strengthens and improves blood vessel health, reducing leakage and capillary fragility, which can lead to eye bags, due to anthocyanins and vitamin P.
Astringent: The astringent properties of bilberry tea tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags, thanks to tannins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried bilberry leaves, simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the bilberry leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the bilberry tea and place them on closed eyes for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom tea helps with eye bags because it contains ruscogenins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. Additionally, butcher's broom is known to improve circulation and strengthen blood vessel walls, which can help prevent the pooling of fluid in the eye area that can cause bags. The tea can be applied topically as a compress or consumed as a beverage to provide these benefits. However, it is important to note that while butcher's broom tea may help reduce the appearance of eye bags, it is not a cure for underlying conditions such as allergies or aging that may contribute to their development.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butcher's broom tea helps reduce eye bags by decreasing inflammation and swelling, thanks to its active constituents, such as ruscogenins and flavonoids.

Venotonic: It strengthens blood vessel walls, reducing leakage into the surrounding tissues, and improves circulation, which minimizes the appearance of eye bags, due to its ruscogenins and saponins content.
Diuretic: Butcher's broom tea promotes fluid elimination, reducing excess fluid accumulation under the eyes, as it contains various compounds that stimulate kidney function.
Antioxidant: The tea's potent antioxidants, like ruscogenins and flavonoids, protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the signs of aging, such as eye bags.
Anti-edematous: Butcher's broom tea inhibits the enzymes responsible for fluid accumulation, thereby reducing eye bags and puffiness, attributed mainly to its ruscogenins content.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried butcher's broom herb, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb, then allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the butcher's broom tea, ensuring they are fully saturated.
Place the soaked cotton pads on closed eyelids for 10-15 minutes, repeating as needed for eye bag relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with eye bags because it contains flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These compounds can help reduce swelling and tighten the skin around the eyes, thereby reducing the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, eyebright tea is rich in antioxidants that can help improve circulation and reduce puffiness. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can help soothe irritated eyes, reduce inflammation, and promote overall eye health. However, it is important to note that while eyebright tea can help with eye bags, it is not a cure-all solution, and other factors such as genetics, lifestyle, and overall health can also contribute to the development of eye bags.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness in eye bags, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and tannins.
Astringent: Eyebright tea has astringent properties that tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags, due to its tannin content.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tea contains antioxidants that protect the skin from damage and help maintain skin elasticity, which is important for preventing and reducing eye bags caused by aging and environmental factors.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tea has antimicrobial properties that help keep the eye area clean and free from infection, which can contribute to the development of eye bags. This is attributed to its active components like phenolic acids and essential oils.
Diuretic: Eyebright tea has diuretic properties that help reduce fluid retention, which can contribute to eye bags. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and other water-soluble compounds.
remedy_recipe: In a heatproof container, combine 1 teaspoon of dried eyebright herb with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 10-15 minutes to allow the herb to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the herb and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the prepared eyebright tea and place them on closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.
Discard the used cotton pads and store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with eye bags because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. The tea is rich in flavonoids and tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of eye bags. Additionally, plantain tea contains vitamin A, which is essential for healthy skin and can improve the overall appearance of the eye area. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a compress can help alleviate eye bags and promote a more refreshed and youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and tannins, which help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area.
Astringent: The astringent properties of plantain tea, due to its tannin content, can help tighten the skin around the eyes and reduce the appearance of eye bags.
Antioxidant: Plantain tea is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and epicatechin, which help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of plantain tea, attributed to compounds like flavonoids and saponins, can help maintain eye area hygiene and prevent infections that could worsen eye bags.

Soothing: Plantain tea has soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and redness, providing relief for individuals with eye bags caused by allergies or fatigue. This soothing effect is attributed to the plant's mucilaginous content and various anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves, rinse them thoroughly, and cut into small pieces.
Place the chopped leaves in a pot, add 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat, simmer for an additional 15 minutes, then remove from heat and let it cool.
Strain the tea into a clean container, discarding the spent plantain leaves.
Soak two cotton balls in the cooled plantain tea, then place them on closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye bags with herbal teas is by using chamomile or green tea bags. After steeping the tea bags in hot water, let them cool down and then place them on your closed eyes for about 15 minutes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in these teas can help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around your eyes. Do this routine regularly for best results. However, keep in mind that while herbal teas can alleviate symptoms, persistent eye bags might be a sign of underlying health issues. Consult a healthcare professional if you have concerns.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye bags that are treated with herbal teas include dark circles and puffy eyes. Certain herbal teas, such as chamomile and lavender, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes. These teas can also help improve circulation, which can reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, the antioxidants found in some herbal teas, such as green tea, can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. To use herbal teas for treating eye bags and related ailments, soak a clean cloth in cooled tea and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes.
